title: rubus × micrandrus
intro: Rubus × micrandrus, also known as the hybrid black raspberry or thimbleberry, is a type of bramble plant that is a cross between the black raspberry and the thimbleberry. The leaves and fruits of the Rubus × micrandrus plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of the plant are attributed to its high content of anthocyanins, ellagic acid, and other polyphenolic compounds, which have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing the risk of heart disease, cancer, and cognitive decline. The plant is often used to make jams, jellies, and teas, and can also be consumed fresh or dried as a supplement, providing a rich source of fiber, vitamins, and minerals.
